heading: Higher grade after 30 years of service: warrant officers and enlisted members
label: 9344 Higher grade after 30 years of service: warrant officers and enlisted members
notes: Historical and Revision Notes1956ActRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)896410:594 (1st proviso, less last 39 words; and last proviso).10:1004 (less 30 words before proviso).(1st proviso, less last 39 words; and last proviso); restated (c) (1st proviso, less last 39 words; and last proviso), ; , .Aug. 21, 1941, ch. 384, § 5June 29, 1948, ch. 708, § 20362 Stat. 1085May 29, 1954, ch. 249, § 19(f)68 Stat. 167(less 30 words before proviso), .June 29, 1948, ch. 708, § 203(e)62 Stat. 1086The words “when his active service plus his service on the retired list totals 30 years” are substituted for the words “upon the completion of thirty years’ [years of] service, to include the sum of his active service and his service on the retired list”, in 10:594 and 1004. The words “under any provision of law”, in 10:594 and 1004; “officer, flight officer, or warrant officer”, in 10:594; and “commissioned, warrant, or enlisted”, in 10:1004; are omitted as surplusage. 10:594 (last proviso) and 1004 (proviso) are omitted as superseded by .section 1372 of this title

Amendments2023—Subsec. (b)(2). struck out “Regular” before “Space Force”.Pub. L. 118–312021—Subsec. (a). , inserted “or the Space Force” after “member of the Air Force”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 923(c)(21)(A)(i)Subsec. (b)(1). , inserted “or the Space Force” after “Air Force”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 923(c)(21)(A)(ii)(I)Subsec. (b)(2). , inserted “or the Regular Space Force” after “Regular Air Force”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 923(c)(21)(A)(ii)(II)Subsec. (b)(3). , inserted “or the Space Force” after “Air Force”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 923(c)(21)(A)(ii)(I)2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 8964 of this title1987— substituted “warrant officers and enlisted members” for “Air Force warrant officers; regular enlisted members” in section catchline, and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Each warrant officer of the Air Force, and each enlisted member of the Regular Air Force, who is retired before or after this title is enacted is entitled, when his active service plus his service on the retired list totals 30 years, to be advanced on the retired list to the grade that is equal to the highest grade in which he served on active duty satisfactorily, as determined by the Secretary of the Air Force.”Pub. L. 100–1801984— substituted “highest grade” for “highest temporary grade”.Pub. L. 98–5251958— struck out “after and before ” after “Secretary of the Air Force”.Pub. L. 85–861September 8, 1940July 1, 1946

children.label: a
children.text: Each retired member of the Air Force or the Space Force covered by subsection (b) who is retired with less than 30 years of active service is entitled, when his active service plus his service on the retired list totals 30 years, to be advanced on the retired list to the highest grade in which he served on active duty satisfactorily (or, in the case of a member of the National Guard, in which he served on full-time duty satisfactorily), as determined by the Secretary of the Air Force.
warrant officers of the Air Force or the Space Force;
enlisted members of the Regular Air Force or the Space Force; and
reserve enlisted members of the Air Force or the Space Force who, at the time of retirement, are serving on active duty (or, in the case of members of the National Guard, on full-time duty).
